close_reason: Converted single-arm match to if-let with early return
description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1425: match tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() { Err(_) => ..., Ok(_) => ... } pattern-matches on Result solely to discard both the Ok and Err payloads; clippy::pedantic single_match_else flags it.

Whether 'if Handle::try_current().is_err()' or 'if let Err(_) = ...' is more readable here is a judgment call — the current match makes the two-branch structure visually clear, which has value when the branches are 10+ lines each. Filing as P3 because the auto-suggested 'if let Err(_)' rewrite is uglier than the original; the right fix is probably to keep the match but extract the two arms to named functions (no_runtime_path / inside_runtime_path), which combines well with avkc.10 (deduplicate the runtime-build boilerplate).
title: get_blocking match on Handle::try_current is single-arm; single_match_else fires
